title: Mika's Big Sleepover Mix-Up
synopsis: When Mika's first sleepover doesn't go as planned, she learns that the best adventures happen when friends work together to solve problems.

pages.text: Mika bounced on her toes. Today was the day! Her best friends Zara and Jamie were coming for her first sleepover ever. "Mom, when will they get here?" Mika asked for the tenth time. "Soon, sweetie," Mom laughed. "Why don't you check your sleepover list again?"

illustration.description: A young girl with curly black hair and big brown eyes bouncing excitedly in a colorful bedroom. She wears purple pajamas with stars. Her mom, a woman with warm brown skin and kind eyes, watches from the doorway with an amused smile. The room has bright pink and purple decorations.
illustration.alt: Mika bouncing excitedly while her mom watches from the bedroom doorway

pages.text: Mika looked at her paper. She had written in purple crayon: 1. Tell scary stories 2. Eat popcorn 3. Stay up ALL NIGHT! 4. Have pancakes for breakfast. "This will be the best sleepover ever!" she said. Just then, the doorbell rang. "They're here!" Mika raced to the door.

illustration.description: A close-up of a hand-written list in purple crayon on white paper, with stars and hearts doodles around the edges. The paper is slightly crumpled from excitement. Bright colors and playful children's handwriting style.
illustration.alt: Mika's colorful sleepover list written in purple crayon with cute doodles

pages.text: Zara stood at the door with her sleeping bag. She had beautiful dark skin and wore her hair in two puff ponytails. Jamie was right behind her, carrying a big backpack. Jamie had freckles and red hair in a messy bun. "Hi Mika!" they both said. "Are you ready for the most amazing sleepover?" Mika grinned. "Let's go!"

illustration.description: Three diverse young girls greeting each other at a front door. Mika has curly black hair, Zara has dark skin and puff ponytails with a bright yellow shirt, and Jamie has red hair in a bun with freckles and a green t-shirt. All have big excited smiles and are carrying sleepover supplies.
illustration.alt: Mika greeting her friends Zara and Jamie at the front door with their sleepover gear

pages.text: The girls set up their sleeping bags in Mika's room. "Let's tell scary stories first!" said Mika. But when she started her ghost story, Zara hid under her sleeping bag. "I don't like scary stories," Zara whispered. "They give me bad dreams." Mika felt silly. She didn't know Zara was scared of ghost stories.

illustration.description: Three sleeping bags arranged in a circle on a bedroom floor. Zara is completely hidden under her bright yellow sleeping bag while Mika looks concerned and Jamie sits cross-legged looking sympathetic. The room is dimly lit with a small nightlight casting gentle shadows.
illustration.alt: Zara hiding under her sleeping bag while Mika tells a scary story

pages.text: "That's okay," said Mika. "Let's make popcorn instead!" The three friends went to the kitchen. But when Mika put the popcorn in the microwave, she pressed too many buttons. POP! POP! BANG! Black smoke came out. "Oh no!" cried Mika. The popcorn was all burnt and yucky.

illustration.description: Three girls standing in a bright kitchen with wisps of black smoke coming from an open microwave. Mika looks worried with her hands on her cheeks, while Zara and Jamie peer curiously at the smoking microwave. The kitchen has cheerful yellow walls and colorful dish towels.
illustration.alt: The girls looking at burnt popcorn smoking in the microwave

pages.text: Mika's mom came into the kitchen. "What happened here?" she asked, opening windows to let the smoke out. "I ruined everything," Mika said sadly. "This is the worst sleepover ever." Zara and Jamie looked at their friend. They could see Mika was really upset. "Don't worry," said Jamie. "We can fix this!"

illustration.description: Mika's mom opening kitchen windows while the three girls stand together. Mika looks sad with drooping shoulders and a pouty expression, while Zara gently pats her back and Jamie has a determined, encouraging expression. Warm kitchen lighting with family photos on the walls.
illustration.alt: Mika looking sad while her friends comfort her and mom opens windows

pages.text: "I have an idea!" said Zara. "My grandma taught me how to make trail mix. We just need nuts and dried fruit." Jamie clapped her hands. "And I brought ingredients for friendship bracelets! We can make them while we eat." Mika looked up. "Really? You want to keep having the sleepover?" "Of course!" said her friends.

illustration.description: The three girls huddled together in the kitchen, with Zara gesturing excitedly and Jamie pulling colorful string from her backpack. Mika's expression is changing from sad to hopeful. Various nuts and dried fruits are scattered on the counter in bright bowls.
illustration.alt: The friends planning new activities together with craft supplies and snacks

pages.text: The girls worked together to make the best trail mix ever. Zara measured the nuts. Jamie added dried cranberries and chocolate chips. Mika mixed it all up in a big bowl. "This is much better than popcorn!" said Mika, munching happily. Then they sat in a circle to make friendship bracelets.

illustration.description: Three girls sitting on the kitchen floor in a triangle formation, surrounded by colorful bowls of trail mix ingredients. Each girl is focused on a different task - measuring, pouring, and mixing. The scene has warm lighting and shows teamwork with bright, happy colors.
illustration.alt: The girls working together to make trail mix in the kitchen

pages.text: "Instead of scary stories, let's tell funny stories!" suggested Jamie. Soon all three girls were giggling. Zara told them about the time her little brother put socks on the cat. Jamie shared how she once brushed her teeth with face cream by mistake. Even Mika laughed about burning the popcorn. "This is so much fun!" she said.

illustration.description: Three girls sitting in their sleeping bags in a cozy bedroom, all laughing with their heads thrown back and big joyful expressions. They're wearing colorful pajamas and have friendship bracelets on their wrists. A small lamp provides warm, golden lighting in the background.
illustration.alt: The girls laughing together in their sleeping bags while telling funny stories

pages.text: The girls tried to stay up all night, but by ten o'clock, they were all yawning. "Maybe we don't need to stay up ALL night," said Mika sleepily. "Just later than usual," agreed Zara. They snuggled into their sleeping bags, whispering and giggling until they fell asleep. Mika smiled. This was perfect.

illustration.description: Three girls in their sleeping bags on the bedroom floor, looking drowsy but happy. The room is lit by a soft nightlight shaped like a moon. They're all wearing their new friendship bracelets and have peaceful, sleepy expressions with half-closed eyes.
illustration.alt: The friends settling down to sleep in their sleeping bags with a gentle nightlight

pages.text: The next morning, Mom woke them up with the smell of pancakes. "Breakfast time!" she called. The girls stumbled to the kitchen, still in their pajamas. There was a stack of fluffy pancakes with syrup and berries. "At least one thing on my list worked perfectly!" laughed Mika. "These pancakes are amazing, Mom!"

illustration.description: Three sleepy girls in colorful pajamas sitting at a breakfast table with a tall stack of golden pancakes topped with berries and syrup. Mika's mom is in the background holding a spatula with a warm smile. Morning sunlight streams through the window.
illustration.alt: The girls enjoying pancakes for breakfast while still in their pajamas

pages.text: As Zara and Jamie packed up their things, Mika felt a little sad. "I'm sorry my sleepover didn't go the way I planned," she said. "Are you kidding?" said Zara. "This was the best sleepover ever!" "We made trail mix, friendship bracelets, and told the funniest stories," added Jamie. "When can we do it again?" Mika hugged her friends tight. Sometimes the best plans are the ones you don't plan at all.

illustration.description: The three girls in a group hug by the front door, all wearing their matching friendship bracelets. Their faces show pure happiness and friendship. Zara and Jamie have their packed bags nearby, and warm morning light fills the entryway with a golden glow.
illustration.alt: Mika hugging her friends goodbye, all wearing their new friendship bracelets

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What was the first thing on Mika's sleepover list?
questions.explanation: Mika's list showed 'Tell scary stories' as number 1, written in purple crayon.

questions.question: Why didn't Zara like the scary stories?
questions.explanation: Zara told Mika that scary stories give her bad dreams, which is why she hid under her sleeping bag.

questions.question: What happened to the popcorn?
questions.explanation: Mika pressed too many buttons on the microwave and the popcorn came out burnt with black smoke.

questions.question: What did the girls make instead of popcorn?
questions.explanation: Zara suggested making trail mix with nuts and dried fruit, which her grandma had taught her to make.

questions.question: How did the girls feel about the sleepover at the end?
questions.explanation: Zara said 'This was the best sleepover ever!' and Jamie asked when they could do it again.

pages.title: Complete Mika's Story
pages.instructions: Fill in the missing words to complete the sentences from the story!

sentences.text: Mika was excited about her first _____ with her friends.
When the _____ got burnt, Mika felt sad.
The girls worked together to make delicious _____.
They told _____ stories instead of scary ones.

pages.title: Sleepover Memory Match
pages.instructions: Match each friend with what they contributed to make the sleepover better!

matches.right: knew how to make trail mix
